feed.title: CEO vs. Employee at 11AM, 3PM,…
caps: linkedin

There is a version of this video where it looks like the founder never sleeps and the employee is taking it easy. That is the joke.

Here is the reality.

Leanna manages mentee escalations, coordinates with title companies and wholesalers, preps and supports weekly calls, analyzes deals, and keeps the operation running smoothly every single day. She is exceptional at what she does, and moments like this remind me how important it is to build a team that truly believes in the mission.

I started Principles of Real Estate after years of driving a bus, wondering if financial freedom was even possible for someone like me. Today, I lead a team, walk investment properties, and help W-2 workers take their first step toward building real wealth through real estate.

What this video really captures is the behind-the-scenes of a growing real estate education business. The energy is different at every hour, but the commitment does not stop.

If you are thinking about how real estate investing could fit into your life, I would love to connect and talk through your first step.

Book a call here: https://calendly.com/bookdeba/road-map-real-estate-investing-property-review-clone-1

#PrinciplesOfRealEstate #DebaDouglas #RealEstateInvesting #W2ToFreedom #WealthBuilder

---
tiktok

CEO vs. Employee energy is NOT the same and this video proves it. 😂

But Leanna? She is a gem. Handling fires, mentee calls, wholesaler calls, and deal analysis all before 7PM. Do not let this reel fool you.

This is what building real wealth behind the scenes actually looks like. I went from driving a bus to leading a team and walking deals daily. The grind is real on both sides.

If you are a W-2 worker ready to start YOUR real estate journey, comment "INVESTOR" 👇

#PrinciplesOfRealEstate #DebaDouglas #RealEstateInvesting #W2ToFreedom #DallasRealEstate

---
instagram

CEO vs. Employee at 11AM, 3PM, and 7PM. 👀

Okay but real talk. Leanna is an absolute rockstar and this video proves it. While I'm out walking houses and chasing deals, she is handling escalations, prepping mentee calls, analyzing deals, AND still finding time to eat (fast food counts). 🙌

This is what building a real business looks like behind the scenes. Two people locked in, playing their roles, and moving the mission forward together.

I went from driving a bus to building a team like this. It did not happen overnight, and it did not happen alone.

If you are a W-2 worker watching this thinking "I want to build something real," this is your sign. Real estate gave me the freedom to lead, create, and grow. It can do the same for you.

Comment "INVESTOR" 👇 and let's map your first step.

#PrinciplesOfRealEstate #DebaDouglas #RealEstateInvesting #W2ToFreedom #DallasRealEstate #BuyAndHold #WealthBuilder #RealEstateCoach #FixAndFlip #BehindTheScenes

---
facebook

CEO life vs. employee life? This video says it all. 😄

But let me be clear: Leanna is one of the most dedicated people on this team. She handles escalations, runs mentee calls, communicates with title companies, analyzes deals, and somehow still manages to eat something in between. She is the real deal, and I am grateful for her every single day.

What this video really shows is what it looks like to build something from the ground up. Two people, fully committed, working different lanes but chasing the same mission. Changing the world, one house at a time.

I started this journey as a bus driver with a dream and zero real estate knowledge. Now I run a team, walk properties, mentor W-2 workers, and help people build generational wealth. That did not happen by accident. It happened because I made a decision and found people who believed in the mission.

If you are ready to take your first step into real estate investing, we would love to walk that road with you.

Comment "INVESTOR" 👇 and let's get you started.

---

feed.title: She walked in nervous. She walked…
caps: youtube

Nicole Redford walked into a Principles of Real Estate class feeling nervous and uncertain. She walked out with $200 toward staging her very first investment property and a clear path forward.

This short clip captures the energy inside a live Principles of Real Estate class with Deba Douglas. From total beginners to people mid-career looking to build long-term wealth, every student in that room showed up ready to learn, and Nicole's win is proof of what is possible when you take that first step.

She put it simply: "I know Deba has all the plans and the success rate. I'm in good hands."

That confidence does not come from nowhere. It comes from a room designed to meet you exactly where you are, break down real strategies in plain language, and give you a roadmap you can actually follow.

Whether you are a W-2 worker curious about buy and hold, fix and flip, or creative finance, this community is built for you.

If you are ready to start your own journey, book a free strategy call and let's map out your first move together.

https://calendly.com/bookdeba/road-map-real-estate-investing-property-review-clone-1
linkedin

The most powerful moment in a beginner investor's journey is not closing the first deal. It is deciding to show up before they feel ready.

Nicole Redford walked into a Principles of Real Estate class nervous, uncertain, and fully present. She left with $200 toward staging her first investment property and, more importantly, with clarity and a plan.

Her words said it best: "I know Deba has all the plans and the success rate. I'm in good hands."

That is the environment we build inside every class. A structured, judgment-free space where W-2 workers and first-time investors can learn real strategies, ask real questions, and take real steps toward financial freedom through real estate.

The barrier to entry in real estate is rarely money. It is almost always confidence and knowledge. When you close that gap, everything changes.

If you are ready to stop waiting and start building, let's talk about your first step.

feed.title: Flips get you capital. Rentals build…
caps: linkedin

Most people know flipping houses can generate income. Fewer understand that rental properties are where lasting wealth is actually built.

This is the core principle Deba Douglas teaches at every Principles of Real Estate session, and the results speak for themselves.

Inside the program, 75% of mentees purchase a property. 89% go on to acquire multiple properties within the same year. One mentee, Josh, completed the program in October and is already on his third property.

The strategy is straightforward: use fix and flip to generate capital, then deploy that capital into buy and hold rentals. The rental is the long game. It's the asset that keeps working while you do.

For W-2 professionals looking to build wealth outside of their paycheck, real estate offers a proven path. But it requires education, community, and a roadmap, not just motivation.

If you're ready to understand what that first step actually looks like for your situation, let's talk.

Book a free property review here: https://calendly.com/bookdeba/road-map-real-estate-investing-property-review-clone-1

#RealEstateInvesting #BuyAndHold #WealthBuilding #PrinciplesOfRealEstate #W2ToFreedom
youtube

Deba Douglas breaks down the real wealth-building play in real estate: rental properties. In this clip from a live Principles of Real Estate session, Deba shares the program stats and explains why flips are just the beginning.

75% of mentees in the program purchase a property. 89% go on to buy multiple properties in the same year. 25% convert into long-term rentals. And mentee Josh, who joined in October, is already closing on his third property.

The strategy Deba teaches is clear: use fix and flip to build your capital base, then move that capital into buy and hold rentals. That's the engine behind real, lasting wealth.

This isn't about getting rich overnight. It's about building a roadmap, following it with intention, and letting your properties work for you over time.

Whether you're a W-2 worker who has never bought an investment property or someone looking to move from flips into a long-term portfolio, Principles of Real Estate meets you where you are.

If you're ready to map out your first deal or next property, book a free strategy session with Deba here: https://calendly.com/bookdeba/road-map-real-estate-investing-property-review-clone-1

The roadmap is real. The results are real. And Deba is pushing every single student toward the big play, buy and hold rental properties.

This is how everyday W-2 workers build long-term wealth. Not overnight, but on purpose.

feed.title: One class. That's all it takes…
caps: youtube

One student came into Principles of Real Estate already owning a rental property. He and his wife had kept their old home when they moved, but they never acquired it with a strategy or a plan. After just his first class, something shifted.

In this clip, he shares how the program is already helping him and his wife see a clear path toward fulfilling her dream of building a real rental portfolio, the right way. He talks about consistency, what he's learning about when to move and when to hold, and how much potential is sitting right in front of them.

This is the heart of what Principles of Real Estate is built to do. Meet you where you are, whether you're starting from zero or you already have a property and just need direction, and give you the tools to move forward with intention.

One class. One consistent step. One dream closer.

If you're ready to stop dabbling and start building, let's map out your real estate plan together.

Book your free strategy call here: https://calendly.com/bookdeba/road-map-real-estate-investing-property-review-clone-1

#PrinciplesOfRealEstate #DebaDouglas #RealEstateInvesting #RentalProperty #BuyAndHold
linkedin

Sometimes the clarity you need arrives in a single session.

One student just completed his first class at Principles of Real Estate, and he came in already owning a rental property with his wife. But that property wasn't acquired with strategy or intention. There was no plan behind it.

After one class, the shift was clear. He reflected on consistency, on learning when to move and when to hold, and on the untapped potential sitting right in front of him. His wife's long-held dream of building a real rental portfolio suddenly had a path forward.

That's what real estate education does. It doesn't just teach you what's next. It helps you recognize the value in what you already have and build on it with intention.

One class. One decision. One consistent step.

If you're sitting on real estate potential and wondering what the next move looks like, let's talk.
